rooms.name: Main House
rooms.description: The formal heart of the Baroo Estate, containing the family bedrooms, principal reception rooms, kitchen and banquet hall.

rooms.name: Baroo's Room
rooms.description: J. Baroo's private room, kept much as they left it. Personal papers, old possessions and evidence of their final days may remain inside.

rooms.name: Parlour
rooms.description: A comfortable but formal reception room used for drinks, family conversations and private meetings away from the larger gatherings.

rooms.name: West Bathroom
rooms.description: A small bathroom serving the western bedrooms and parlour, tucked into one of the quieter corners of the house.

rooms.name: Acacia Bedroom
rooms.description: A traditional guest bedroom near the estate kitchen, suited to a long-standing visitor or member of the wider Baroo circle.

rooms.name: Estate Kitchen
rooms.description: The main working kitchen of the estate, used to prepare formal meals and store the household's serving equipment, provisions and wine.

rooms.name: Waiting Room
rooms.description: A modest sitting room between the kitchen and banquet hall where guests or staff can wait before being admitted to dinner.

rooms.name: Banquet Hall
rooms.description: The estate's grand dining room, used for the evening meal and the formal gathering before the reading of J. Baroo's will.

rooms.name: Central Bathroom
rooms.description: A centrally located bathroom shared by the parlour, Banksia Bedroom and the main internal corridor.

rooms.name: Banksia Bedroom
rooms.description: A centrally positioned guest bedroom with convenient access to the parlour, bathroom and eastern side of the house.

rooms.name: Morning Room
rooms.description: A bright informal sitting room used for breakfast, correspondence, reading and quieter daytime conversations.

rooms.name: Eucalyptus Bedroom
rooms.description: A secluded guest bedroom on the eastern side of the main corridor, opening towards the verandah.

rooms.name: East Bathroom
rooms.description: A narrow bathroom serving the eastern bedrooms and verandah side of the house.

rooms.name: Widow's Suite
rooms.description: The Surviving Spouse's large private suite, separated from the other bedrooms and opening directly onto the East Verandah.

rooms.name: East Verandah
rooms.description: A covered outdoor passage running along the eastern side of the house, connecting several rooms with the gardens.

rooms.name: Front Verandah
rooms.description: The broad entrance verandah overlooking the driveway and grounds, where guests arrive and move between the house and estate.

rooms.name: Baroo's Retreat
rooms.description: A private room away from the main household where J. Baroo could work, rest or exist without the pressures of the main house.

rooms.name: Cottage Kitchen
rooms.description: A smaller kitchen serving the cottage, suitable for private meals, tea and guests staying away from the main house.

rooms.name: Washroom
rooms.description: A compact utility and bathroom space shared by the cottage occupants.

rooms.name: Lounge
rooms.description: The cottage's principal sitting room, more relaxed and intimate than the formal reception rooms of the main house.

rooms.name: Cottage Verandah
rooms.description: A sheltered verandah overlooking the nearby garden, connecting the retreat and cottage rooms with the grounds.

rooms.name: Garden Room
rooms.description: A detached garden pavilion used for private conversations, reading or escaping the activity of the main house.

rooms.name: Conservatory Garden
rooms.description: A cultivated garden beside the cottage, with dense plantings, formal paths and several places where a conversation could be had in private.

rooms.name: Estate Grove
rooms.description: The largest wooded section of the property, with winding paths, mature trees and poor sightlines between the house and lake.

rooms.name: Lake
rooms.description: A broad ornamental lake at the southern end of the estate, isolated from the house and partially screened by trees.

rooms.name: Driveway Garden
rooms.description: A landscaped garden lining the approach to the main house, giving arriving guests their first view of the Baroo Estate.
